goal: Implement GAP-005 structured decision-request governance as a narrow runtime cockpit slice.
objective: Make decision-request/v1 an emitted, reachable, read-only governance packet with machine-readable intent, authority, default option, evidence references, freshness, expiry, and stale-state classification.
feedbackSignal: GAP-005 says structured decision requests are partial: intent, authority, human escalation, and stale-state handling must be machine-readable, not prose-only.
rootOperationalFailure: The repository already publishes a decision-request/v1 schema, but the manifest marks it not_yet_emitted and there is no reachable CLI/deep module that can produce the packet.
plannedDurableImprovement: Add a decision-request deep module and registry command that emits decision-request/v1 packets, updates the runtime packet manifest from not_yet_emitted to emitted, and validates stale or expired decisions deterministically.
forbiddenFiles: generated or cache artifacts not directly owned by this slice
nonGoals: Do not refresh GitHub, CI, review, Linear, or CodeRabbit external state.
Do not make decision-request a closeout success proof.
Do not mutate tracker, PR, branch, or repo state from the command.
Do not broaden harness next into a new multi-command surface in this slice.
Do not edit unrelated Project Brain WIP currently untracked in the worktree.
implementationApproach: Create typed decision-request packet contracts in src/lib/decision-request/types.ts.

Create a pure builder that validates required fields, parses option identity, rejects duplicate option ids, checks defaultOptionId membership, classifies expired decisions as stale, and emits staleState entries.

Create a read-only CLI that accepts --json, --intent, --default-option, repeated --option id=Label, repeated --tradeoff id=Text, --authority, --evidence, --expires-at, --generated-at, --status, --freshness, --producer, --request-id, --escalation-target, --escalation-channel, and --escalation-reason.

Use one canonical option grammar: each --option value is id=label; each --tradeoff value is id=text and attaches to the matching option id. Unknown tradeoff option ids, duplicate option ids, and missing option labels fail closed with usage errors.
Emit a required escalation object with required, targetRole, channel, reason, and requestedAt so human or operator routing stays machine-readable instead of prose-only.

Apply stale-state precedence deterministically: computed expiry at or before generatedAt overrides caller-provided status/freshness and normalizes status to expired plus freshness to stale; explicit non-current freshness or expired status then emits staleState; otherwise status/freshness remain caller-provided defaults.
Register the command through the command registry and agent handoff rail with safe/read-only metadata.
Update schema, example, and manifest to runtimeStatus emitted with a real type source and validator support.
Document the command in CLI reference surfaces without claiming it proves closeout readiness.
Record a goal receipt and state update that keeps push, PR, CI, review-thread, Linear, and merge-readiness truth separate.

expectedOutputContract.escalation: required object with required, targetRole, channel, reason, and requestedAt
expectedOutputContract.stalePrecedence: expiresAt <= generatedAt normalizes status=expired and freshness=stale before staleState derivation

validationGates: pnpm vitest run src/commands/decision-request.test.ts -- --runInBand equivalent not required; tests must include duplicate option id, unknown tradeoff id, expired/current conflict normalization, and missing escalation field coverage
acceptanceCriteria: decision-request/v1 can be emitted through a real command registry path.
The runtime packet manifest no longer marks decision-request/v1 as not_yet_emitted.
decision-request/v1 includes a machine-readable escalation object with required, targetRole, channel, reason, and requestedAt.
Expired or explicitly stale decision requests include staleState and cannot be mistaken for current closeout proof.
Default option validation fails closed when it does not match an option id.
Duplicate option ids and tradeoffs for unknown option ids fail closed with deterministic usage errors.
Expired decisions normalize status/freshness before staleState derivation even when caller input claims current/open state.
Command catalog metadata exposes decision-request as safe, read-only, agent-usable governance handoff.
Schema/example validation covers the emitted packet shape.
Goal receipts record local validation separately from push/PR/CI/review/Linear readiness.
riskControls: Keep the command read-only and do not write packets to disk in this slice.
Keep the module deep under src/lib/decision-request instead of inflating command registry files.
Treat external state and tracker truth as evidence references only, never as refreshed proof.
Preserve unrelated dirty and untracked worktree files.
blockedConditions: Cannot validate the command registry path.
Cannot isolate unrelated worktree WIP from the slice.
